hero.body: Every career change weighs two discomforts against each other: the anxiety of what could go wrong, and the slow disappointment of staying where you no longer fit. Most people pick the disappointment, one year at a time.
hero.bodyEmphasis: Cairnly is one assessment to look at that choice properly: what would change actually look like for you, and would it be worth it?
hero.reassurance: Done in one sitting. No subscription. No account left dangling.

pillars.titleA: Four things we got tired of

cards.title: Not another personality test
cards.body: Personality tests give you a four-letter label. Cairnly gives you specific job titles: your best fits, runner-ups, and one out-of-the-box option you wouldn't have considered.

cards.title: Interactive, not static
cards.body: Every assessment comes with a coaching chat. If a recommendation doesn't land, push back. Your results adapt until they actually fit.

cards.title: Future-aware
cards.body: Every suggested role includes an honest read on how AI is set to reshape it. We tell you what's likely to grow and what's likely to shrink.

cards.title: Honest, not flattering
cards.body: Real reality checks on challenges, trade-offs, and the skills you'd need to develop. We're not here to cheerlead. We're here to help you choose well.

howItWorks.subtitle: Five steps, from where you are now to actually landing the job. Concrete career paths that fit who you've become, not who you thought you'd be.

steps.body: A guided set of questions about your background, skills, work style, values, and what's actually energized or drained you so far. Designed to capture what matters for career fit. Not just personality traits.
steps.screenshotAlt: Assessment question about working in teams

steps.body: Seven AI workflows, built and tuned with working career coaches, read your responses end to end. They build your personality and values profile, match you to specific roles, score AI-impact, and write personalized justifications.
steps.extra: Not a ChatGPT prompt you could write yourself. Not a vibe-coded weekend project.
steps.extraLink: See every step in the methodology

steps.body: Discuss your results one-on-one. Ask follow-ups, explore specific roles in depth, push back when something doesn't fit. You'll get honest answers about trade-offs and next steps. Not flattery.
steps.screenshotAlt: AI coaching session with your personality profile

steps.body: Your finished career-change dashboard, refined by the chat: personality and values analysis, every career recommendation, salary data, AI-impact ratings, and concrete next steps.
steps.screenshotAlt: The Cairnly dashboard with career signature and top matches

steps.body: Pick the roles that resonate. Cairnly finds live openings for them. When you apply, your resume and cover letter are tailored using everything Cairnly already knows about you. No prompting required.
steps.extra: The job-landing features are in beta and free to unlock.
steps.screenshotDesc: Coming once the beta job-landing flow ships.

methodology.titleA: Not a formula. Not freestyle AI.
methodology.intro: Most personality frameworks sort you into a box, then tell you what people in that box typically do. Generic AI tools skip the science entirely and improvise. Cairnly does neither.

oldCard.title: The old approach
oldCard.p1: You answer a fixed set of questions. A formula sorts you into one of a small number of types. The result describes what people of that type typically do, not what would suit you, with your history, in your life.
oldCard.p2: The labels are catchy. The science behind some of them is contested. And whether you're one letter or another doesn't tell you whether to be a UX designer or a recruiter.

newCard.title: Cairnly's approach
newCard.p1: Our assessment is built on the validated parts of established psychometric research, the work that actually predicts career fit, values alignment, and work preferences.
newCard.p2: On top of that, we layer the part formulas can't capture: your actual career history, what's energized you, what's drained you, where you're trying to go. Frontier AI, guided by working career coaches, reads all of it together and builds an actionable plan for this person. Not for a type.

methodology.engineTitle: Seven AI workflows. One coach-tuned pipeline.
methodology.engineSubtitle: Every step has a purpose. Every prompt was co-written with working career coaches, so the output reads like advice from a pro, not a chatbot.
methodology.closer: The validated parts of psychometric research, the practical instincts of real career coaches, and modern AI that can hold all of it in mind at once. So you get advice that's actually about you.

chatRefine.eyebrow: The chat that comes with your dashboard
chatRefine.titleA: If something doesn't fit, tell it.
chatRefine.intro: Every Cairnly assessment comes with an interactive coaching chat, but not the kind that bills you monthly to be your friend. It's a refinement loop. A way to challenge the AI when something doesn't ring true, so the final result actually does.

staticPdf.title: A static PDF
staticPdf.body: Reads you once, prints an answer, walks away. If a recommendation feels wrong, your only option is to ignore it.

cairnly.label: Cairnly
cairnly.title: A Cairnly dashboard
cairnly.body: Comes with a chat that has one job:
cairnly.bodyContinued: . Push back. Say "actually, I'd hate that one" or "tell me more about runner-up #2." Your dashboard incorporates your feedback. Then you take it and go.

subChat.title: A subscription chat
subChat.body: Stays available forever. Designed to keep you logging in. Great for company. Less great for closing the question.

chatRefine.closer: It's the difference between an assessment about you and one built with you. And once it's right, it stays right.

comparison.titleQuote: what should I actually do next?
comparison.intro: Some tools box you into a personality type. Others charge you monthly to keep exploring. Cairnly is built for one job: turn who you actually are into specific roles you could go land. Once. Done.

forkDivider.oldSub: More of what you already know.
forkDivider.newSub: The one you'd pick now, with what you know.

finalCTA.titleA: The direction you'd choose now, with the life you've
finalCTA.footer: Built with career coaches. Backed by frontier AI. Honest about both.

whyBuilt.p1: They're inherited from a decision someone made at 16, before they knew themselves well enough to make it. A general direction shaped a major. A major shaped a first job. A first job shaped the next ten. By the time you look up, you might be a department manager when you'd have made a better entrepreneur, or a builder, or something you've never even considered.
whyBuilt.p2: We built Cairnly because the tools for asking that question properly didn't exist. Personality tests give you a label. Coaching takes months and costs thousands. Free quizzes give you what you paid for. So we built one assessment, honest about its methods, that takes who you actually are now and gives you specific roles to go look at on Monday. No subscription, no roster of upsells. One clear answer, then we get out of your way.

report.title: The Career Uncertainty Report
report.desc: Why so many capable people feel stuck in the wrong career, and the data behind what we built.

workflow.engineDetail: 7 AI-WORKFLOWS · Months of work · Multiple LLMs · Coached and tuned at every step
workflow.reportDetail: Top 3 matches · Runner-up roles · Out-of-the-box · Dream-job feasibility · AI-impact ratings · Salary ranges · Personalized match · Next steps · Done in ~90s · Refined by your coaching chat

items.q: Isn't this just ChatGPT in a wrapper?
items.a: No. The assessment is built on validated psychometric research and refined with working career coaches. The AI is the engine that processes your specific history and responses against that foundation, it isn't improvising. Generic AI tools skip the science. We don't.

items.q: How is this different from career coaching?
items.a: Coaching is excellent for executing a direction once you've picked one. But it costs €80–€280 a month indefinitely while you figure out what that direction is. Cairnly is the clarity step that comes before coaching is worth paying for. Roughly half the price of a single coaching session, for the full picture.

items.q: How is this different from monthly AI career platforms?
items.a: Subscription platforms are designed to keep you exploring. We're designed to give you a finished answer. After two months at €25/month, you've paid more than a Cairnly assessment, and you're still being billed. We give you one clear answer, you take it and act on it.

items.q: What if the recommendations don't feel right?
items.a: Push back. The coaching chat exists precisely for that. If a role doesn't fit, tell it, and your results adapt. By the time everything's finalized, your feedback is already in it.

GDPR-compliant. Your data is stored on EU-based servers, and the AI providers we use for the assessment and coaching chat (Anthropic and Google) don't train their models on it, per their commercial API terms. Payments are handled by Stripe (we never see your card), and you can permanently delete your account and everything in it with one click, any time. Your data is yours.
One sitting. Most people complete the assessment and chat in under an hour. Your full dashboard is yours immediately.

other.label: Something else…
other.placeholder: Tell us in a few words what brings you here
other.thanks: Thanks, that genuinely helps us make Cairnly better.

hero.titleA: You're good at your job. That doesn't make it a
hero.body: Competent and quietly unsure is not a contradiction, it's more common than you think. Cairnly is one assessment that looks at who you've become, not the role you grew into, and asks:
hero.bodyEmphasis: if you could choose again, knowing what you know now, would you choose this?

whyBuilt.p1: Some careers are grown into rather than chosen. You were good at something, so you got more of it. More responsibility, more of the same kind of work, a title that fits your skills but maybe not the person behind them. Competence is a quiet current: by the time you look up, you're the person everyone relies on, for work you're no longer sure you'd pick. That question deserves better than a gut feeling.

hero.titleA: AI will not affect all jobs the same. Understand
hero.body: AI is rewriting some careers and barely touching others. Cairnly scores every path it suggests on how AI is set to reshape it, so the real question becomes:
hero.bodyEmphasis: which direction still has room for you in the coming years, and do you actually want it?

whyBuilt.p1: They're built on assumptions that stopped being safe. You picked a field, got good at it, and planned on it carrying you for decades. Then the ground started moving. Some roles will grow, some will shrink, most will change shape, and the honest answer for yours is buried under equal parts hype and doom. Sorting that out deserves better than a headline.

hero.body: Kids, burnout, a move, a loss, a restart: the ground shifts and the old fit stops fitting. Cairnly is one assessment that takes where you actually are now, not where you were at 25, and asks:
hero.bodyEmphasis: what would work look like if it fit the life you have today?

whyBuilt.p1: They're chosen once and then assumed to keep fitting forever. But lives don't hold still. Kids arrive, health interrupts, priorities shift, and what a job needs to give you at 40 is rarely what you asked of it at 25. Most people carry a career shaped around a life they no longer live, and the tools to re-ask the question honestly were never built for them.

hero.titleA: You can't pick the right direction until you
hero.body: Cairnly starts with a serious assessment of who you've become: your strengths, your history, what actually drives you. The career answers are built on that foundation, which is why the first question isn't about jobs at all:
hero.bodyEmphasis: who are you now, and what do you actually want from the next chapter?

whyBuilt.p1: They're chosen before the chooser is fully formed. You picked a direction before you knew your own strengths, your patterns, what gives you energy and what quietly drains it. Twenty years of living generates a lot of self-knowledge, but almost nobody sits down to turn it into an honest picture of who they've become. Without that picture, every career question is a guess.

hero.titleB: picked the next twenty years for you.
hero.body: You chose a general direction, business, engineering, healthcare, and it quietly chose your major, your first job, and every one after. Cairnly is one assessment to ask the question that 16-year-old never could:
hero.bodyEmphasis: with the life you've lived since, what would you actually pick now?
